what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view F2 ("Settings \u2014 consolidated") in the design blueprint ledger. It declares the UI addresses the surface covers, the platforms and feature areas it represents, its planned status, its unresolved journey placement, and the components, lint command, and token source it should build to.
observations: home_journey is "_unrouted" and the file sits in the _unrouted directory
journeys and cross_journeys arrays are empty even though the comment says they carry the web
build_to points to a single component ("settings"), a design-lint command, and a token source file
the provenance note says it was built by a Claude Code session and warns "never invent an address"
standouts: empty journeys/cross_journeys despite the directory name and description implying routing is central
a concrete, enumerated list of settings sub-addresses
build_to couples the surface to both lint and token-generation artifacts
represents two distinct configuration panels in one consolidated surface
conventions: Follows the ledger surface-spec schema: id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, build_to
Uses ui:// URI scheme for address references
Includes a _what provenance/comment field at the top
References external build commands and a canonical token file
concerns: journeys and cross_journeys are empty while the description says they are the address-refs that carry the web, so the spec appears incomplete
status "planned" means the surface is not yet active
build_to command path is relative and its resolution depends on repository context
no explicit mapping from individual addresses to components or journeys
notes: This is a planned, un-routed surface spec for a consolidated Settings view, enumerating settings addresses but not yet wiring them into journeys.
questions: Why are journeys and cross_journeys empty if they are meant to carry the web?
Which component or sub-view maps to each ui://settings address?
What does "B3" refer to in this context?
How does the design_lint command relate specifically to this surface?
How are RHM-config and SM-panel intended to coexist within one consolidated settings surface?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a generated surface spec with a journey spine and address-refs; the actual content provides addresses and build targets but lacks journey/cross-journey wiring, so it is a partial surface spec awaiting routing.
apparent_themes: separation of surface specs from build/lint artifacts
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a single consolidated Settings surface that covers multiple settings sub-pages (analytics, brain, RHM, roles, swap, etc.) and tie them to a design-system build pipeline, while deferring the journey topology to a later step.
novelty: The explicit "never invent an address" provenance note and the "_unrouted" home_journey marker make the file's uncertainty transparent; the build_to block also couples the surface to both lint and token generation.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete, enumerated set of settings-related UI addresses and a declared intent to consolidate RHM-config and SM-panel settings into one surface, plus build-target metadata that a fused system can use to resolve which components and design tokens apply to the settings area.
summary_for_embedding: A planned, un-routed surface specification (F2) for a consolidated Settings view listing ui://settings addresses, representing RHM-config and SM-panel, targeting the settings component with design-lint and token build sources.
intention_for_embedding: To record the intended scope and address set of a consolidated Settings surface before journey routing is assigned.
